stories.title: City: Greenville Zoo’s leopard tranquilized, returned after escaping exhibit
stories.summary: A four-year-old Amur leopard named Irina escaped her exhibit at the Greenville Zoo in South Carolina on Thursday morning, prompting the evacuation of zoo staff and guests. She was successfully tranquilized and returned to her den by zoo emergency response teams at approximately 9:20 a.m. The zoo will remain closed while officials investigate the incident.

stories.title: FDA panel votes to place popular peptide BPC-157 on compounding list
stories.summary: A US FDA panel has voted to place the peptide BPC-157 on the list of compounds that can be made by licensed pharmacies, a move that could pave the way for its use in human clinical trials. The peptide has been studied for its potential therapeutic benefits, including healing wounds and reducing inflammation. Its addition to the compounding list is expected to facilitate further research into its medical applications.

stories.title: St Gallen vs Benfica LIVE Score Updates in UEFA Europa League Match
stories.summary: In the UEFA Europa League Preliminary Round, St Gallen is facing Benfica in the first leg of their match at the AFG Arena stadium. St Gallen is competing in the tournament for the eighth time in its history, but has yet to reach the decisive stages. The match will be refereed by Luca Pairetio, with the Swiss club missing key players due to injury and suspension.

stories.title: Modric, 40, signs new AC Milan deal
stories.summary: Luka Modric, 40, has signed a one-year contract extension with AC Milan, following a season where the team finished fifth in Serie A. The Croatian midfielder joined Milan last summer on a one-year deal with an option to extend, after leaving Real Madrid where he spent 13 years. Modric is now set to continue giving his all for the team in the upcoming season.

stories.title: Saudis must recognise Israel for nuclear deal, says Trump
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has stated that Saudi Arabia must recognise Israel in order for a nuclear deal between the two countries to proceed, with the deal dependent on Riyadh joining the Abraham Accords. The condition was reportedly discussed with Saudi leaders on multiple occasions, with Trump stating that if they do not join the accords, the deal is off. The nuclear deal, which has not been released, is intended for non-military use.

stories.title: Russell proud of resilience after string of problems
stories.summary: George Russell cut his race deficit from 66 to 25 points over three recent grands prix, telling reporters he’s still making progress despite setbacks. A Mercedes investigation found an electrical power issue cost him half a second in qualifying and hurt his race pace in Belgium. Russell now faces the Dutch Grand Prix on August 24 aiming to build on the gains made so far.

stories.title: Ukraine's ousted defence minister insists on being reinstated
stories.summary: Ukraine’s defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov, 35, has refused an alternative government role and demanded reinstatement after his dismissal last week. President Volodymyr Zelensky offered Fedorov other posts including deputy prime minister amid mass protests. Fedorov, credited with modernising the army and leading anti-corruption efforts, remains a key figure in Kyiv’s war strategy.

stories.title: Corsair’s PC case with a panoramic glass design is $70 off
stories.summary: A PC case with a panoramic glass design is now available at a discounted price of $119.99, a $70 reduction from its usual price. The Corsair Frame 4500X RS case supports large motherboards and graphics cards, and features a unique Infinirail system for easy fan installation. This case is currently on sale at Amazon, Best Buy, and Corsair's official website.

stories.title: Google and Tesla shares plunge as AI spending rattles markets
stories.summary: Google's parent company Alphabet saw its share price drop by over 7% on Thursday, while Tesla's stock fell 13.5% as investors reacted to the companies' massive spending on artificial intelligence. Alphabet now expects to spend up to $205 billion this year, mainly on AI projects and infrastructure, a $15 billion increase from its previous estimate. This significant investment has raised concerns about the potential return on these investments.

stories.title: FCC Poised to Hand Broadcasters Long-Sought Win in Abolishing Station Ownership Cap as Brendan Carr Probes Tension Between TV Groups and Platforms
stories.summary: The Federal Communications Commission is set to vote on August 6 to eliminate the station ownership cap, a regulation limiting the number of US TV stations a single entity can own, a move that could bring broadcasters a long-sought victory. The cap was first enacted for radio stations in 1941 and has been in place for over 80 years. This decision comes as FCC Chairman Brendan Carr probes tensions between TV groups and platforms over sports rights and streaming.

stories.title: Venice Chief Alberto Barbera on Landing Robert Pattinson Film ‘Primetime’ at the Last Minute and Lack of U.S. Studios at the Festival: They Are ‘Experiencing an Identity Crisis’
stories.summary: Venice Film Festival chief Alberto Barbera has successfully assembled a star-studded lineup despite a significant decrease in films from US studios and streamers, with 18 new projects from top international directors set to premiere. This comes as Barbera attributes the lack of US studio involvement to an "identity crisis" within the industry. The festival will continue to showcase a diverse range of films, with the world premiere of the Oasis reunion documentary "Don't Look Back in Anger" among the highlights.

stories.title: Lobbying firm pressed for details about payments to journalists for favourable stories
stories.summary: A lobbying firm, CT Group, is facing calls to reveal details of payments made to journalists for favourable stories, following a four-month investigation that exposed the practice as a threat to democracy. The firm, co-founded by Lynton Crosby, has been condemned by MPs, senior lobbyists, and transparency campaigners, who are demanding an overhaul of lobbying rules. The practice has been denounced as an attack on the ethics of journalism and democracy.

stories.title: ‘Percy Jackson’ Season 3 Drops First Opening Scene Footage at Comic-Con as Walker Scobell Tells Fans the New Episodes Will Stay True to ‘Titan’s Curse’ Book
stories.summary: At San Diego Comic-Con, Disney+ premiered the opening scene of Season 3 of "Percy Jackson and the Olympians", adapting Rick Riordan's book "The Titan's Curse". The new season will follow Percy Jackson, Grover Underwood, and their fellow campers at Camp Half-Blood, with Walker Scobell reprising his role as Percy. The third season is set to stay true to the original book's storyline.

stories.title: Wolves training cancelled after Arokodare refuses to leave pitch
stories.summary: Wolves striker Tolu Arokodare halted a training session on Monday after refusing to leave the pitch. The 25-year-old Nigeria international demanded a move to Fiorentina despite Wolves accepting a bid from Trabzonspor. The club confirmed the session was cut short by new manager Cesar Peixoto.

stories.title: Tesla’s robotaxi promises are clashing with reality
stories.summary: Tesla’s robotaxi program drove 380,000 unsupervised miles across six cities in two states as of yesterday’s earnings call. CEO Elon Musk acknowledged scaling efforts while stressing safety, noting growth is flat despite claims of progress. The company faces scrutiny over its technology’s safety record amid limited mileage gains.

stories.title: Houthi attacks raise fears of wider Middle East conflict and more global economic damage
stories.summary: Houthi attacks on Red Sea shipping threaten to disrupt the Europe-Asia trade route through the Bab El Mandeb strait. Two Saudi vessels were hit by missiles this week, escalating tensions after the 2022 ceasefire with Saudi Arabia collapsed. US Central Command has conducted 12 consecutive nights of airstrikes on Iranian coastal targets.

stories.title: Yemen's Houthis attack Saudi tanker as US launches more Iran strikes
stories.summary: US warships struck Iranian-backed Houthi targets in Yemen for a 12th straight night Wednesday, while the militia claimed it hit a Saudi oil tanker in the Red Sea. The Saudi-run agency reported one vessel caught fire but gave no casualties. Washington warned the Houthis and Tehran of swift retaliation if attacks continue.

stories.title: Modi Caught Off Guard as India’s Economic Failings Inflame Young People
stories.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi was busy trying to put out other fires when a student movement suddenly exploded. There are no easy solutions to the problems behind it.

stories.title: ‘Odyssey’ Popcorn Buckets Help Fuel a $100 Million Market
stories.summary: Once, a tub of popcorn was just a tub of popcorn. Now, ‘collectible concession vessels’ could make one chain more than $100 million.

stories.title: Israel Welcomes Trump’s Demand for Diplomatic Ties With Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: Israeli officials greeted the possibility of normalized ties, but analysts said the U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal nonetheless remained bad for Israel.

stories.title: Chaos Breaks Out at Border Crossings With Iraq
stories.summary: Videos verified by The New York Times show border crossings in southern Iraq becoming the latest target in the hostilities in the Middle East.

stories.title: The only way to stop petrol pump thieves | Brief letters
stories.summary: Petrol pump thieves are exploiting a loophole in the payment system, with many stations releasing pumps before payment is confirmed. In the UK, it's estimated that over £1 million is stolen from petrol pumps each year. To prevent such thefts, some experts are advocating for a change in policy, requiring a cash or card deposit before releasing the pump.

stories.title: BBC Inside Science
stories.summary: The US military will begin screening military personnel aged 30 and older for testosterone deficiency, with those found to have low levels offered voluntary hormone replacement therapy. The move aims to maintain a "healthy, capable, and decisively dominant fighting force". The new policy is set to be implemented by the US Department of Defence.

stories.title: NASA’s MAVEN Illuminates New Understanding of Auroras at Mars
stories.summary: NASA’s MAVEN mission revealed Mars experiences auroras through the same particle-driven process as Earth, despite weaker magnetic fields. Findings published Thursday in Nature Communications show the Dungey cycle accelerates charged particles into Mars’ atmosphere on smaller scales. Data from the now-concluded mission continues to shape future Mars exploration efforts.

stories.title: Crews Move Artemis IV Liquid Hydrogen Tank
stories.summary: Crews at NASA's Michoud Assembly Facility in New Orleans have successfully transported the 130-foot-tall Artemis IV liquid hydrogen tank to a detached test building on May 15, 2026. The tank will form part of the core stage for the SLS rocket, providing thousands of gallons of super-cold propellant to one of four RS-25 engines. This milestone marks a significant step forward in the development of the Artemis IV mission.

stories.title: Risk of Parkinson’s disease may increase with prolonged exposure to road traffic noise
stories.summary: A new study has found that prolonged exposure to road traffic noise may increase the risk of Parkinson's disease, with a 3% higher risk for every 11.5 decibel rise in noise level at the most exposed facade. The study, which included 3.1 million Danish participants aged 40 and over, followed them for 18 years and suggests that having a quiet part of the home may mitigate this risk. The findings, published in Jama Neurology, highlight the potential long-term health consequences of exposure to road traffic noise.

stories.title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
stories.summary: Agency scientists are advising against allowing widespread use of several peptide injections. Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the health secretary, has said people should be free to experiment.
